detail.reasoning: AST-25818 audio dropout at 00:69:12 is a deliberate creative blackout confirmed by production annotations covering the flagged range. Per RQ-CREATIVE-02, this flag is not a defect; the tentpole release for TTL-78218 proceeds tomorrow with marketing locked and CVAA obligations in the US.

The Long Quiet (TTL-44220) releases in 2 days in APAC and EU, territories that are not CVAA-covered, so the 0.8 s caption drift at 00:37:26 is not a legal hard block. However, the defect is a timed-text issue on a standard-tier title with marketing locked and no in-house caption repair capability available before premiere. Per policy, this requires a delay to allow vendor redelivery or internal fix, so the release is delayed.

Northwind (TTL-84611) premieres in 3 days, marketing is locked, and the territory list includes US where CVAA applies. The lip-sync error (audio leads picture by ~4 frames at 00:29:56) is a major defect, but audio defects are eligible for in-house repair under policy RQ-FIX-04, which can be completed within the remaining schedule. No creative annotations cover the flagged timecode, so the defect is not an intentional creative choice.

AST-64321 is a video-essence colour-metadata mismatch flagged at 00:06:06. Per RQ-FIX-04, picture defects baked into the essence require vendor redelivery; in-house repair is not possible. TTL-22909 premieres in 8 days, outside the 3-day minor-defect window in RQ-MINOR-03, so a corrected package can be accepted. No creative annotations cover the flagged timecode, and the issue is not an accessibility (CVAA) requirement, so vendor_redelivery is the correct remediation.

The flagged compression artefacts at 00:85:26 on AST-30777 are a major video defect. Glass Harbour (TTL-75837) is a standard-tier title with 20 days to premiere in APAC and EU, where CVAA does not apply. In-house repair is not possible for baked-in picture defects (RQ-FIX-04), and vendor redelivery would require ~5 days, exceeding the remaining window. Given the defect’s severity and the lack of creative annotations covering the timecode, this is a commercial escalation per RQ-BOARD-05.

AST-28159 is a tentpole title with one day to premiere in UK/EU (non-CVAA). The flagged loudness drift (1.4 LU above spec at 00:21:40) is a minor audio defect. In-house repair is not feasible within the release window, and vendor redelivery would miss the marketed premiere. Per policy RQ-BOARD-05, this commercial decision escalates to the release board.

The flagged 22 s digital silence at 00:70:30 is explicitly annotated as an intentional creative choice (director’s cut, no score or room tone) that covers the flagged range. Policy RQ-CREATIVE-02 states that such annotations override detector flags; therefore this is not a defect. Title TTL-87874 is a standard-tier release with 2 days until premiere in UK/EU, marketing is locked, and no CVAA accessibility obligations apply, so waiving and releasing is compliant.

TTL-21997 is a tentpole title releasing in the US in 3 days with CVAA accessibility obligations. The QC flag is a caption timing drift (~800 ms over 14 events) which constitutes a caption defect under RQ-CVAA-01. No creative annotations cover the flagged timecode. Because the defect is legally blocking and the schedule does not allow in-house repair before premiere, the release must be delayed to ensure compliance.

Paper Cities (TTL-47186) is a tentpole title premiering in 8 days across UK/EU, where CVAA does not impose a hard sync block. The QC flag is an audio-sync offset of 120 ms from 00:55:18, a major defect but not accessibility-critical in these territories. Policy RQ-FIX-04 confirms in-house audio repair is feasible within the window, so expediting an internal fix keeps the release on schedule without vendor delay.

AST-50799 is a major colour-metadata mismatch between the package descriptor and video essence. The title (TTL-37421, Aster & Vale) is a tentpole release with 20 days until premiere, outside the 3-day patch window. Picture essence defects cannot be repaired in house (policy RQ-FIX-04), so vendor redelivery is required. No creative annotations cover the flagged timecode, and the defect is not an accessibility hard block.

Northwind (TTL-32960) is a tentpole title with a major dropped-frame judder flag at 00:87:17 in AST-67249. The premiere is tomorrow; marketing is locked and the CVAA accessibility rules apply in the US/CA/UK territories. No creative annotations cover the flag. In-house repair is not feasible within the window, and vendor redelivery would miss the premiere. Per RQ-BOARD-05, this is a commercial decision requiring release-board escalation rather than unilateral delay.

Title TTL-36579 (Paper Cities) is a standard-tier release with 2 days until premiere and marketing locked. The audio integrated loudness flag (-21.4 LKFS vs. -27/-23 spec) is a minor spec deviation that can be repaired in house within 2 days per RQ-FIX-04. However, the title is CVAA-covered in the US and CA, and the release board policy RQ-BOARD-05 requires a commercial decision for any defect that could affect accessibility compliance with fewer than 5 days to premiere. Escalating to the release board.

TTL-60423 (Paper Cities) releases in 3 days to US/CA/UK with CVAA obligations. The QC flag shows total audio loss at 00:87:18, a major defect. Policy RQ-FIX-04 confirms in-house audio repair is possible within 2 days, but given the 3-day window and marketing lock, delaying the release is required to ensure a compliant asset. No creative-annotation override applies to total audio loss.

TTL-44639 is a US-title with CVAA obligations and the flagged issue is a caption timing drift (~800 ms across 14 events). Policy RQ-CVAA-01 classifies caption defects as a legal hard block; no severity override is permitted. With 8 days to premiere and marketing not yet locked, the only compliant action is to delay the release to allow in-house repair or corrected vendor delivery.

Title TTL-48517 (Sixteen Winters) is a tentpole US release with CVAA obligations, 20 days to premiere, and marketing locked. The lip-sync error (audio leads picture by ~4 frames at 00:63:48) is a major audio defect per policy RQ-FIX-04, but in-house audio repair is permitted and can be completed within the window. No creative annotations cover the flagged timecode, so the defect is not an intentional creative choice. Therefore, expedite_internal_fix is selected.

AST-94014 is a major video essence defect (colour metadata mismatch) on TTL-50112, ‘The Long Quiet’. The title premieres tomorrow, marketing is locked, and no territories impose accessibility obligations. Policy RQ-FIX-04 states picture essence defects require vendor redelivery and cannot be repaired in house; policy RQ-MINOR-03 allows delaying the release when remediation exceeds the 3-day window. Therefore the release is delayed and the originating vendor will supply a corrected package.

The flagged judder at 00:09:29 on AST-30240 is not covered by the only creative annotation (handheld camera shake from 00:33:00–00:33:40). No policy clause excuses a major video defect this close to premiere in a CVAA-covered territory, so the video-eng queue must remediate. However, the title is tier-standard, marketing is locked, and there are only 2 days until release; vendor turnaround is infeasible. Therefore the asset is expedited for internal repair before premiere.

The flag is a minor audio loudness drift (+1.4 LU) at 00:60:55 on AST-34520 for TTL-58419, ‘The Long Quiet’. The title is standard tier, premiering in 3 days across US & LATAM with CVAA obligations and locked marketing. Policy RQ-FIX-04 confirms in-house audio repair is possible within 2 days, and RQ-PREF-00 prefers in-house fix over vendor redelivery or delay. Therefore, expedite_internal_fix is selected to repair the audio loudness drift in time for the premiere.

The flagged 22 s digital silence at 00:50:05 is covered by a creative annotation confirming it is an intentional ‘audio blackout for dramatic effect’. Policy RQ-CREATIVE-02 classifies such intentional creative choices as no-defect, so the asset is cleared for release. The title’s tier is standard, territories are UK/EU (no CVAA obligations), and marketing is not locked, so no further remediation is required.

TTL-44342 is a tentpole title with a premiere in 20 days and US & LATAM territories, both CVAA-covered. In-house repair is feasible within the remaining window, so expedite_internal_fix is the remediation.

Major lip-sync error (audio leads by ~4 frames) at 00:09:45 on tentpole title TTL-87012 one day from premiere. Policy RQ-BOARD-05 requires a commercial decision when a major defect cannot be fixed in house and there is insufficient time for vendor redelivery on a tentpole title with marketed premiere. Escalating to the release board.

The flagged colour-metadata mismatch is a major video defect that cannot be repaired in house (policy RQ-FIX-04). The title premieres in 2 days and marketing is locked, so a waiver is not permissible. Vendor redelivery is required, even though the defect is cosmetic and does not affect playout.

AST-78645 shows a major lip-sync error (audio leads picture by ~4 frames at 00:63:48) on tentpole title TTL-48517, which is subject to US CVAA accessibility obligations and has 20 days until premiere. Production annotations do not cover the flagged timecode, so the defect is not creative intent. In-house audio repair is feasible within the release window per RQ-FIX-04, and expediting an internal fix avoids the longer vendor redelivery timeline while mitigating commercial risk for a tentpole premiere.

With only one day remaining, the compliant action is to delay_release until a corrected package can be supplied.

Title TTL-39312 (Northwind) premieres in 2 days with marketing locked and CVAA obligations in US/CA/UK. The flagged 22 s digital silence at 00:50:05 on AST-15342 is covered by a post-supervisor annotation marking it as a deliberate audio blackout for dramatic effect. AST-45949 is a timed-text defect (caption timing drift ~800 ms) in a CVAA-covered territory (US) 20 days before premiere. CVAA rules (RQ-CVAA-01) forbid waiving caption defects regardless of severity or schedule. The minor-severity rule (RQ-MINOR-03) does not override accessibility hard-block policy. In-house repair is feasible within the remaining window, so expedite_internal_fix is selected.
